heading: Federal-State partnership for intercity passenger rail

children.label: A
children.text: a State (including the District of Columbia);

children.label: B
children.text: a group of States;

children.label: C
children.text: an Interstate Compact;

children.label: D
children.text: a public agency or publicly chartered authority established by 1 or more States;

children.label: E
children.text: a political subdivision of a State;

children.label: F
children.text: Amtrak, acting on its own behalf or under a cooperative agreement with 1 or more States;

children.label: G
children.text: a federally recognized Indian Tribe; or

children.label: H
children.text: any combination of the entities described in subparagraphs (A) through (G).

children.heading: .—Intercity rail passenger transportation
children.label: 2 .—Intercity rail passenger transportation
children.text: The term “intercity rail passenger transportation” has the meaning given the term in section 24102.

children.label: A
children.text: the main rail line between Boston, Massachusetts and the District of Columbia;

children.label: B
children.text: the branch rail lines connecting to Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, Springfield, Massachusetts, and Spuyten Duyvil, New York; and

children.label: C
children.text: facilities and services used to operate and maintain lines described in subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: .—Grant Program Authorized
children.label: b .—Grant Program Authorized
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation shall develop and implement a program for issuing grants to applicants, on a competitive basis, to fund capital projects that reduce the state of good repair backlog, improve performance, or expand or establish new intercity passenger rail service, including privately operated intercity passenger rail service if an eligible applicant is involved;.1So in original. The semicolon preceding the period probably should not appear.1
A project to replace, rehabilitate, or repair infrastructure, equipment, or a facility used for providing intercity passenger rail service to bring such assets into a state of good repair.

A project to improve intercity passenger rail service performance, including reduced trip times, increased train frequencies, higher operating speeds, improved reliability, expanded capacity, reduced congestion, electrification, and other improvements, as determined by the Secretary.
A project to expand or establish new intercity passenger rail service.
A group of related projects described in paragraphs (1) through (3).
The planning, environmental studies, and final design for a project or group of projects described in paragraphs (1) through (4).

children.label: A
children.text: make selections consistent with the Northeast Corridor Project Inventory published pursuant to subsection (e)(1), unless when necessary to address materially changed infrastructure or service conditions, changes in project sponsor capabilities or commitments, or other significant changes since the completion of the most recently issued Northeast Corridor Project Inventory; and

children.label: i
children.text: are in compliance with section 24905(c)(2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: identify funding for the intercity passenger rail share, the commuter rail share, and the local share of the eligible project before the commencement of the project;

children.label: i
children.text: for which Amtrak is not the sole applicant;

children.label: ii
children.text: that improve the financial performance, reliability, service frequency, or address the state of good repair of an Amtrak route; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that are identified in, and consistent with, a corridor inventory prepared under the Corridor Identification and Development Program pursuant to section 25101; and

children.label: I
children.text: effects on system and service performance, including as measured by applicable metrics set forth in part 273 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations);

children.label: II
children.text: effects on safety, competitiveness, reliability, trip or transit time, greenhouse gas emissions, and resilience;

children.label: III
children.text: anticipated positive economic and employment impacts, including development in areas near passenger stations, historic districts, or other opportunity zones;

children.label: IV
children.text: efficiencies from improved connections with other modes; and

children.label: V
children.text: ability to meet existing or anticipated demand;

children.label: ii
children.text: the degree to which the proposed project’s business plan considers potential private sector participation in the financing, construction, or operation of the proposed project;

children.label: iii
children.text: the applicant’s past performance in developing and delivering similar projects, and previous financial contributions;

children.label: I
children.text: the legal, financial, and technical capacity to carry out the project;

children.label: II
children.text: satisfactory continuing access to the equipment or facilities; and

children.label: III
children.text: the capability and willingness to maintain the equipment or facilities;

children.label: v
children.text: if applicable, the consistency of the project with planning guidance and documents set forth by the Secretary or otherwise required by law;

children.label: vi
children.text: whether the proposed project serves historically unconnected or underconnected communities; and

children.label: vii
children.text: any other relevant factors, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: A
children.text: not less than 45 percent of the amounts appropriated for grants under this section for projects not located along the Northeast Corridor, of which not less than 20 percent shall be for projects that benefit (in whole or in part) a long-distance route; and

children.label: B
children.text: not less than 45 percent of the amounts appropriated for grants under this section for projects listed on the Northeast Corridor project inventory published pursuant to subsection (e)(1).
identifies capital projects for Federal investment, project applicants, and proposed Federal funding levels under this section;

specifies the order in which the Secretary will provide grant funding to projects that have identified sponsors and are located along the Northeast Corridor, including a method and plan for apportioning funds to project sponsors for the 2-year period, which may be altered by the Secretary, as necessary, if recipients are not carrying out projects in accordance with the anticipated schedule;

takes into consideration the appropriate sequence and phasing of projects described in the Northeast Corridor capital investment plan developed pursuant to section 24904(a);2So in original. Probably should be “section 24904(b);”.2
is consistent with the most recent Northeast Corridor service development plan update described in section 24904(d);3So in original. Probably should be “section 24904(a)(3);”.3

takes into consideration the existing commitments and anticipated Federal, project applicant, sponsor, and other relevant funding levels for the next 5 fiscal years based on information currently available to the Secretary; and
is developed in consultation with the Northeast Corridor Commission and the owners of Northeast Corridor infrastructure and facilities.

children.heading: Federal Share of Total Project Costs.—
children.label: f Federal Share of Total Project Costs.—

children.heading: .—Total project cost
children.label: 1 .—Total project cost
children.text: The Secretary shall estimate the total cost of a project under this section based on the best available information, including engineering studies, studies of economic feasibility, environmental analyses, and information on the expected use of equipment or facilities.

children.heading: .—Federal share
children.label: 2 .—Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of total costs for a project under this section shall not exceed 80 percent, except as specified under paragraph (4).

children.heading: .—Treatment of amtrak revenue
children.label: 3 .—Treatment of amtrak revenue
children.text: If Amtrak is an applicant under this section, Amtrak may use ticket and other revenues generated from its operations and other sources to satisfy the non-Federal share requirements.

children.heading: Letters of Intent; Phased Funding Agreements.—
children.label: g Letters of Intent; Phased Funding Agreements.—
A
children.text: announces an intention to obligate, for a major capital project under this section, an amount from future available budget authority specified in law that is not more than the amount stipulated as the financial participation of the Secretary in the project; and

children.label: i
children.text: is not an obligation of the Federal Government; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is subject to the availability of appropriations for grants under this section and subject to Federal laws in force or enacted after the date of the contingent commitment.

children.label: i
children.text: the project is highly rated, based on the evaluations and ratings conducted pursuant to this section and the applicable notice of funding opportunity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Federal assistance to be provided for the project under this section is more than $80,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: establish the terms of participation by the Federal Government in the project;

children.label: ii
children.text: establish the maximum amount of Federal financial assistance for the project;

children.label: iii
children.text: include the period of time for completing the project, even if such period extends beyond the period for which Federal financial assistance is authorized;

children.label: iv
children.text: make timely and efficient management of the project easier in accordance with Federal law; and

children.label: v
children.text: if applicable, specify when the process for complying with the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) and related environmental laws will be completed for the project.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: A phased funding agreement under this paragraph obligates an amount of available budget authority specified in law and may include a commitment, contingent on amounts to be specified in law in advance for commitments under this paragraph, to obligate an additional amount from future available budget authority specified in law.

children.heading: .—Statement of contingent commitment
children.label: ii .—Statement of contingent commitment
children.text: The agreement shall state that the contingent commitment is not an obligation of the Government.

children.heading: .—Interest and other financing costs
children.label: iii .—Interest and other financing costs
children.text: Interest and other financing costs of efficiently carrying out a part of the project within a reasonable time are a cost of carrying out the project under a phased funding agreement, except that eligible costs may not be more than the cost of the most favorable financing terms reasonably available for the project at the time of borrowing. The applicant shall certify, to the satisfaction of the Secretary, that the applicant has shown reasonable diligence in seeking the most favorable financing terms.

children.heading: .—Failure to carry out project
children.label: iv .—Failure to carry out project
children.text: If an applicant does not carry out the project for reasons within the control of the applicant, the applicant shall repay all Federal grant funds awarded for the project from all Federal funding sources, for all project activities, facilities, and equipment, plus reasonable interest and penalty charges allowable by law or established by the Secretary in the phased funding agreement. For purposes of this clause, a process for complying with the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) that results in the selection of the no build alternative is not within the applicant’s control.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Crediting of funds received
children.label: v .—Crediting of funds received
children.text: Any funds received by the Government under this paragraph, except for interest and penalty charges, shall be credited to the appropriation account from which the funds were originally derived.

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.label: i
children.text: a copy of the phased funding agreement or the proposed letter;

children.label: ii
children.text: the criteria used under subsection (d) for selecting the project for a grant award; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of how the project meets such criteria.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into phased funding agreements under this subsection that contain contingent commitments to incur obligations in such amounts as the Secretary determines are appropriate.

children.heading: .—Appropriations required
children.label: B .—Appropriations required
children.text: An obligation or administrative commitment may be made under this section only when amounts are appropriated for such purpose.

children.heading: .—Availability
children.label: h .—Availability
children.text: Amounts appropriated for carrying out this section shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: .—Grant Conditions
children.label: i .—Grant Conditions
children.text: Except as specifically provided in this section, the use of any amounts appropriated for grants under this section shall be subject to the grant conditions under sections 22903 and 22905.

children.heading: .—Annual Report on Phased Funding Agreements and Letters of Intent
children.label: j .—Annual Report on Phased Funding Agreements and Letters of Intent

children.text: a proposal for the allocation of amounts to be available to finance grants for projects under this section among applicants for such amounts;
evaluations and ratings, as applicable, for each project that has received a phased funding agreement or a letter of intent; and

recommendations for each project that has received a phased funding agreement or a letter of intent for funding based on the evaluations and ratings, as applicable, and on existing commitments and anticipated funding levels for the next 3 fiscal years based on information currently available to the Secretary.
providing funding to public entities for the development of service development plans selected under the Corridor Identification and Development Program;
facilitating and providing guidance for intercity passenger rail systems planning; and
providing funding for the development and refinement of intercity passenger rail systems planning analytical tools and models.
